metadata.name: DocSamplePreview
metadata.description: Embedded preview of a generated jsonui-doc HTML artifact. Renders a hand-pasted static HTML snapshot of docs/screens/json/learn/hello-world.spec.json inside a sandboxed iframe so visitors to /tools/doc can see what the CLI actually produces without leaving the page. Zero props: the HTML payload is a string constant baked into the web extension file at author time, deliberately avoiding a build-time dependency on the Python CLI from the Next.js bundle.

notes: Intentionally propless. The preview HTML is a snapshot of the live Hello World generated doc — it never varies per-instance on /tools/doc, and introducing a `src` or `html` prop would invite drift between the CodeBlock tab (raw spec JSON) and this tab (generated output).
If a future page needs a second inlined preview, fork this component (e.g. DocSamplePreviewFirstScreen) rather than adding a prop; the point of the contract is that one component == one snapshot.
Leaf component. Any children passed in the Layout JSON are ignored — the iframe owns its own rendered document.

components.description: Root wrapper that hosts the sandboxed iframe and applies the max-height / scroll styling.

example.description: Zero-attribute usage inside a Layout JSON. Exactly one consumer is expected in v1: docs/screens/layouts/tools/doc.json, inside the sample-preview tab panel.

notes: Rendering contract — the web converter at jsonui-doc-web/src/components/extensions/DocSamplePreview.tsx MUST render: <iframe srcDoc={RAW_HTML} title={TITLE} className="doc-sample-preview" sandbox="allow-same-origin" /> where RAW_HTML is a string constant at the top of the same file. The iframe carries a `title` attribute for accessibility (screen readers); the sandbox attribute prevents the embedded doc from running scripts or navigating the parent frame.

Authoring the snapshot — regenerate RAW_HTML by running `jsonui-doc generate spec docs/screens/json/learn/hello-world.spec.json -o /tmp/hello-world-sample.html --format html` then pasting the file contents as a template literal into DocSamplePreview.tsx. Do NOT wire this into a build step; doing so would couple the Next.js build to the Python CLI, which is explicitly what this contract is designed to avoid.

Styling — CSS (globals.css or a colocated module) clamps the iframe to `max-height: 800px` with internal scroll via `overflow: auto` on the iframe element, `width: 100%`, rounded corners matching the CodeBlock aesthetic, a 1px border, and a light neutral background so long HTML stays visually bounded inside the page.

No interactivity — no click handlers, no event bindings, no dynamic state. If /tools/doc ever needs a live re-generator demo, that belongs in a separate, clearly-named component (e.g. DocSamplePreviewLive) with its own spec.

Why snapshot-based — the /learn/hello-world spec is the site's stable reference fixture; it changes rarely and any change is caught by `jui verify`. Pasting rather than generating keeps the web bundle small, avoids bringing Shiki-for-HTML-escaping into the runtime path, and makes the preview bit-identical to what a reader would get from running the CLI locally.
Registered in .jsonui-doc-rules.json componentTypes.screen as 'DocSamplePreview' (required before any screen spec references the type).
Spec-first discipline — this spec MUST validate before `jui g converter --from doc-sample-preview.component.json` runs; the converter is generated by jsonui-implement, not by define.
